quoteFirst: On September 23, 1907, a rabbit weighing 1,757 grams was fed at
quoteLast: with 0.1 gram of the same barium acetate.
title: Barium: A Cause of the Loco-Weed Disease
author: Crawford, Albert C. (Albert Cornelius), 1869-1921

quoteFirst: Now and again by some violent expedient, such as fancying we heard an accident, or pretending that we had run over a man, or that Mount Vesuvius in a state of eruption was distinctly visible on our left, we succeeded in getting a window down for a minute or two; but from 7.15 p.m. until
quoteLast: we were mercilessly smashed and stifled in a compartment which was not a first-class carriage, but a tin of compressed humanity.
title: Dagonet Abroad
author: Sims, George R. (George Robert), 1847-1922

quoteFirst: —A train leaves the Ramleh station at Alexandria at 7.40 A.M., and reaches Aboukir at
quoteLast: , returning from Aboukir at 4.42 P.M. It is a short walk northwards from the station to the temple of Zephyrion discovered by Daninos Pasha in 1891.
title: The Egypt of the Hebrews and Herodotos
author: Sayce, A. H. (Archibald Henry), 1845-1933
